articles.headline: India Unveils 'Vision 2047: Advanced Manufacturing Hub' Initiative
articles.explainer:
• The Prime Minister launched a new national initiative aimed at transforming India into a global advanced manufacturing hub by 2047, coinciding with 100 years of independence.
• The initiative focuses on integrating cutting-edge technologies like AI, IoT, robotics, and additive manufacturing into traditional industries.
• Key pillars include skill development for future industries, robust R&D infrastructure, and attracting significant foreign and domestic investment.
• It seeks to enhance India's competitiveness in critical sectors such as semiconductors, electric vehicles, aerospace, and pharmaceuticals.
• Special economic zones with advanced manufacturing capabilities and incentives for green manufacturing are also part of the plan.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Industrial policy
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Vision 2047: India's target for becoming a developed nation.
Advanced Manufacturing Hub initiative components: AI, IoT, robotics, skill development, R&D.
Key sectors targeted: semiconductors, EVs, aerospace, pharmaceuticals.
Role of Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes in boosting manufacturing.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's strategy to leverage advanced manufacturing for economic growth and job creation, linking it to the 'Make in India' initiative.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in integrating Industry 4.0 technologies into India's manufacturing sector.
Evaluate the potential impact of such initiatives on India's global supply chain resilience and export competitiveness.
Examine the role of government policy, skill development, and R&D in fostering an advanced manufacturing ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration depicting smart factories with robots and AI interfaces, symbolizing advanced manufacturing.
articles.trivia: India's manufacturing sector contributes approximately 17% to the nation's GDP, with ambitious targets for significant growth.

articles.headline: Canada Designates Lawrence Bishnoi Gang as Terrorist Organisation
articles.explainer:
• The Canadian government officially designated the Lawrence Bishnoi Gang as a terrorist organization, citing its involvement in transnational organized crime, violence, and threats against Indian Canadians.
• This move follows increased diplomatic pressure from India and a growing concern over the gang's alleged activities, including extortion, drug trafficking, and targeted killings.
• The designation allows Canadian law enforcement agencies to impose stricter measures, including asset freezes, travel bans, and enhanced surveillance against the group and its affiliates.
• India had repeatedly raised concerns about the gang's operations from Canadian soil, particularly its alleged links to extremist elements and cross-border criminal networks.
• The decision is seen as a significant step in bilateral cooperation between India and Canada on security matters, aiming to curb organized crime and terrorism.
gsSubTopics: Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: Lawrence Bishnoi Gang: Designated as a terrorist organization by Canada.
Reasons for designation: Transnational organized crime, violence, threats against Indian Canadians.
Implications of designation: Asset freezes, travel bans, enhanced surveillance.
Context: India-Canada bilateral security cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of transnational organized crime groups operating from foreign soil on India's internal security and diplomatic relations.
Discuss the challenges and mechanisms for international cooperation in combating organized crime and terrorism, with specific reference to India-Canada relations.
Examine the role of diplomatic pressure and intelligence sharing in addressing cross-border criminal activities.
Evaluate the effectiveness of terrorist designations by foreign governments in curbing the activities of such groups.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing Canada and India, connected by lines representing international cooperation against crime.
articles.trivia: Interpol plays a crucial role in facilitating international police cooperation and combating transnational crime.

articles.headline: Renowned Filmmaker Yashwant Sardeshpande Passes Away at 62
articles.explainer:
• Veteran Indian filmmaker Yashwant Sardeshpande passed away at the age of 62 after a prolonged illness, leaving behind a legacy of critically acclaimed works.
• Sardeshpande was known for his distinctive narrative style, often exploring social realism and human relationships with profound depth and sensitivity.
• His filmography includes several regional language films that garnered national awards and international recognition, highlighting diverse cultural nuances of India.
• He was a recipient of multiple National Film Awards for his contributions to Indian cinema, particularly in screenwriting and direction.
• His demise marks a significant loss for the Indian film fraternity and the broader cultural landscape.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
prelimsPoints: Yashwant Sardeshpande: Renowned Indian filmmaker.
Known for: Social realism, human relationships, regional language films.
Awards: Multiple National Film Awards.
Contribution: Significant impact on Indian cinema, especially regional films.
articles.imageDescription: Black and white portrait of a thoughtful man, symbolizing the late filmmaker.
articles.trivia: The National Film Awards, established in 1954, are among the most prestigious film awards in India.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds State's Power in Regulating Online Gaming
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, upheld the constitutional validity of state laws empowering governments to regulate and ban online gaming involving stakes.
• The ruling clarified that online games of 'skill' might still fall under state legislative competence if they involve monetary stakes and have potential social impact.
• The Court emphasized the public order and morality aspects, allowing states to frame laws to prevent addiction and financial losses associated with online gambling.
• This decision provides clarity to the burgeoning online gaming industry and state governments grappling with regulatory frameworks.
• It differentiates between games of pure skill without stakes and those involving monetary transactions, which can be regulated under state gambling laws.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court ruling on online gaming: Upholds state's power to regulate/ban if stakes involved.
Distinction: Games of skill vs. games of chance, especially with monetary stakes.
Constitutional aspect: State legislative competence under public order and morality.
Impact: Clarity for online gaming industry and state governments.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on online gaming for India's federal structure and the balance of power between Union and States.
Discuss the socio-economic challenges posed by unregulated online gaming and how state regulations aim to address them.
Critically examine the distinction between 'games of skill' and 'games of chance' in the context of emerging digital platforms and their legal implications.
Suggest a balanced regulatory framework for online gaming in India that promotes innovation while safeguarding public interest.
articles.imageDescription: Image of a gavel on a law book, symbolizing a Supreme Court judgment, with abstract digital gaming elements.
articles.trivia: Gambling is primarily a subject under the State List (List II) of the Seventh Schedule of the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: India Achieves Significant Milestone in Renewable Energy Capacity Addition
articles.explainer:
• India announced achieving a major milestone by crossing 200 GW of installed renewable energy capacity, excluding large hydro, well ahead of its initial targets.
• This achievement underscores the nation's aggressive push towards green energy and its commitment to climate change mitigation.
• Solar and wind power constitute the largest share of this capacity, driven by supportive government policies like Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes and competitive bidding.
• The accelerated deployment of renewable energy is crucial for India to meet its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
• The focus now shifts towards grid modernization, energy storage solutions, and green hydrogen integration to ensure grid stability and further decarbonization.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India's renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro) crosses 200 GW.
Major contributors: Solar and Wind power.
Government policies: PLI schemes, competitive bidding.
Relevance: Meeting NDCs under Paris Agreement, climate change mitigation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's rapid growth in renewable energy capacity and its implications for energy security and environmental sustainability.
Discuss the challenges associated with integrating high shares of intermittent renewable energy into the national grid and the solutions being explored (e.g., storage, smart grids).
Evaluate India's role in global climate action, specifically its progress towards its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) and future targets.
Examine the economic opportunities and policy framework required to further accelerate India's energy transition, including green hydrogen.
articles.imageDescription: Panoramic view of a large solar power farm and wind turbines under a clear sky.
articles.trivia: India is the third-largest emitter of greenhouse gases globally, making its renewable energy transition critical for global climate efforts.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded with both nations reaffirming their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defense cooperation, including joint military exercises, technology transfer, and maritime security partnerships.
• Economic cooperation saw new proposals for investment in India's infrastructure, digital transformation, and clean energy sectors.
• Both sides expressed concerns over regional stability and emphasized the importance of multilateralism and adherence to international law.
• The dialogue also covered collaboration in critical and emerging technologies, supply chain resilience, and cooperation in third countries.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue: Annual event, focus on Indo-Pacific.
Key areas of cooperation: Defense, economic investment, digital transformation, clean energy, critical technologies.
Shared vision: Free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
Relevance: Multilateralism, international law, regional stability.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-Japan Special Strategic and Global Partnership in shaping the geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the various pillars of cooperation between India and Japan, including defense, economy, and technology, and their strategic implications for India.
Examine how India and Japan are collaborating to address common challenges such as maritime security, supply chain resilience, and climate change.
Evaluate the role of India-Japan cooperation in strengthening multilateral frameworks like the Quad and promoting regional stability.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan side-by-side, with a stylized map of the Indo-Pacific region in the background.
articles.trivia: Japan is one of India's largest providers of Official Development Assistance (ODA), funding major infrastructure projects like the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV-TD)
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducted a crucial test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD).
• This test involved a precise landing experiment, simulating the return of a reusable launch vehicle from space, a key step towards India's future space capabilities.
• The RLV-TD program aims to develop a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch vehicle, significantly reducing the cost of space access.
• This technology is vital for future missions, including human spaceflight (Gaganyaan) and deploying large satellite constellations.
• The successful test positions India among a select few nations developing advanced reusable space technology.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD: Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator.
Purpose: Reduce cost of space access, develop two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) vehicle.
Latest test: Precise landing experiment, simulating re-entry.
Significance: Crucial for future missions like Gaganyaan, positions India in advanced space tech.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of reusable launch vehicle technology for India's space program and its implications for commercial space exploration.
Analyze how ISRO's RLV-TD program aligns with India's broader vision for technological self-reliance and global leadership in space.
Examine the economic and scientific benefits of reducing the cost of access to space through reusable technology.
Evaluate the challenges and future prospects for India in developing and deploying fully reusable launch systems.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV-TD) making a controlled descent.
articles.trivia: The first successful test of the RLV-TD's hypersonic flight experiment was conducted in May 2016.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at its latest meeting.
• The decision was driven by signs of easing retail inflation and a need to support economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
• RBI indicated a cautious stance, monitoring food inflation and global commodity prices, which remain potential risks.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to achieving the medium-term target for consumer price index (CPI) inflation of 4% within a band of +/- 2%.
• Experts anticipate that this pause will provide stability to lending rates and encourage investment, contributing to a gradual economic recovery.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy.
prelimsPoints: RBI MPC decision: Repo rate maintained.
Reason: Easing retail inflation, support economic growth, global uncertainties.
RBI's inflation target: 4% CPI with +/- 2% band.
Impact: Stability in lending rates, potential for investment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing the RBI's monetary policy decisions, particularly the trade-off between inflation control and economic growth.
Discuss the effectiveness of the repo rate as a tool for managing inflation and liquidity in the Indian economy.
Examine the role of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India's economic governance and its impact on financial markets.
Critically evaluate the challenges faced by the RBI in achieving its inflation targets while navigating global economic volatility and domestic supply-side pressures.
articles.imageDescription: Close-up of Indian currency notes with a magnifying glass, symbolizing economic analysis.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: Parliament Passes Bill to Amend Forest Conservation Act for Strategic Projects
articles.explainer:
• Parliament passed the Forest (Conservation) Amendment Bill, 2025, which aims to streamline approvals for diverting forest land for strategic national projects.
• The Bill seeks to exempt certain categories of land, including forest land up to 100 km along international borders and land used for national security projects, from strict conservation rules.
• Proponents argue the amendments are crucial for infrastructure development, national security, and facilitating ease of doing business.
• Critics express concerns that the relaxed provisions could lead to significant deforestation and undermine environmental protection efforts.
• The Act, originally enacted in 1980, mandated prior approval from the central government for any non-forest use of forest land.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Forest (Conservation) Amendment Bill, 2025: Passed by Parliament.
Purpose: Streamline approvals for strategic national projects on forest land.
Exemptions: Land up to 100 km along international borders, national security projects.
Arguments: Infrastructure, national security vs. environmental concerns.
Original Act: Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the Forest (Conservation) Amendment Bill, 2025, in the context of balancing environmental protection with developmental and national security imperatives.
Discuss the potential ecological and social impacts of diluting forest conservation laws, particularly concerning tribal rights and biodiversity.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing environmental safeguards and compensatory afforestation mechanisms in mitigating the adverse effects of forest diversion.
Propose alternative approaches or stricter implementation strategies that can reconcile developmental needs with robust forest conservation.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic showing a balance scale with 'Development' on one side and 'Environment' on the other, with a forest background.
articles.trivia: The Forest (Conservation) Act of 1980 was enacted to check deforestation and ensure that forest land is not diverted for non-forest purposes without central government approval.

articles.headline: Government Launches National Digital Health Mission (NDHM) Phase 2
articles.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry officially rolled out Phase 2 of the National Digital Health Mission (NDHM), expanding its reach and functionalities across the country.
• Phase 2 focuses on integrating more healthcare providers, diagnostic labs, and pharmacies into the digital ecosystem, along with advanced telemedicine services.
• Key features include a more robust Health ID system, enhanced data privacy and security protocols, and interoperability standards for seamless data exchange.
• The mission aims to create a comprehensive digital health infrastructure, improving access, efficiency, and quality of healthcare services.
• Special emphasis is placed on leveraging AI and machine learning for predictive analytics and personalized healthcare solutions.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: National Digital Health Mission (NDHM) Phase 2 launched.
Focus: Integrating more providers, advanced telemedicine, robust Health ID.
Goal: Comprehensive digital health infrastructure, improve access, efficiency, quality.
Technology: AI and ML for predictive analytics, personalized healthcare.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of the National Digital Health Mission (NDHM) to transform healthcare delivery in India, addressing issues of access, affordability, and quality.
Analyze the challenges involved in implementing a large-scale digital health infrastructure, including data privacy, cybersecurity, and digital literacy.
Examine how NDHM aligns with other government initiatives like Ayushman Bharat and the broader vision of digital India.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to digital health services across rural and urban areas and for vulnerable populations.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract graphic of interconnected medical symbols and digital lines, representing digital health network.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), launched in 2021, is the current name for the National Digital Health Mission.

articles.headline: New Integrated Border Management System (IBMS) Deployed Along Western Border
articles.explainer:
• India successfully deployed an advanced Integrated Border Management System (IBMS) along critical stretches of its western border with Pakistan.
• The IBMS integrates state-of-the-art surveillance technologies, including thermal imagers, night-vision devices, radar, and underground sensors, with a centralized command and control system.
• The system aims to enhance real-time situational awareness, detect infiltrations more effectively, and reduce human reliance in challenging terrains.
• This deployment is part of a broader strategy to fortify India's borders against cross-border terrorism, smuggling, and illegal immigration.
• Training for border security forces (BSF) personnel has been intensified to operate and maintain the sophisticated technology.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security.
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: IBMS: Integrated Border Management System deployed on western border.
Technologies used: Thermal imagers, night-vision, radar, underground sensors.
Objective: Enhance real-time situational awareness, detect infiltration, reduce human reliance.
Purpose: Fortify borders against terrorism, smuggling, illegal immigration.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of border security challenges for India and how technological solutions like IBMS are addressing them.
Analyze the effectiveness of integrated border management systems in curbing cross-border terrorism and illegal activities along sensitive borders.
Examine the challenges in deploying and maintaining advanced surveillance technologies in diverse and often hostile border terrains.
Suggest policy measures and international cooperation strategies to further enhance India's border management capabilities.
articles.imageDescription: Infrared camera view of a border fence at night, with digital overlay of sensor data.
articles.trivia: The Border Security Force (BSF) is India's primary border guarding organization on its borders with Pakistan and Bangladesh.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Comprehensive UN Security Council Reforms at General Assembly
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its strong call for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) during the ongoing UN General Assembly session.
• India emphasized the need for an expanded Security Council that reflects contemporary global realities, including an increase in both permanent and non-permanent members.
• The permanent representative highlighted the disproportionate representation of the global South and the lack of permanent representation for Africa and Latin America.
• India's consistent demand for a permanent seat aligns with its growing global stature and its contributions to international peace and security.
• The push for reforms aims to make the UNSC more democratic, representative, effective, and legitimate in addressing 21st-century challenges.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India's stance on UNSC: Call for comprehensive reforms, expansion of permanent and non-permanent members.
Reason: Reflect contemporary global realities, address underrepresentation of Global South (Africa, Latin America).
India's demand: Permanent seat in UNSC.
Objective: Make UNSC more democratic, representative, effective, legitimate.
mainsPoints: Discuss the arguments for and against the reform of the UN Security Council, with particular reference to India's aspirations for a permanent seat.
Analyze how the current structure of the UNSC impacts its effectiveness in addressing contemporary global challenges and representing diverse geopolitical interests.
Examine the implications of a reformed UNSC for global governance and the balance of power among nations.
Evaluate the diplomatic strategies employed by India to garner support for its permanent membership bid and the challenges it faces.
articles.imageDescription: The United Nations building in New York, with flags of member states, symbolizing global diplomacy.
articles.trivia: The UN Security Council has 5 permanent members (China, France, Russia, UK, US) and 10 non-permanent members elected for two-year terms.

articles.headline: COP30 Preparations Underway, India Emphasises Climate Justice
articles.explainer:
• As preparations for the 30th Conference of Parties (COP30) to the UNFCCC gather pace, India reiterated its stance on climate justice.
• India emphasized the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in climate negotiations.
• The nation called upon developed countries to fulfill their historical responsibilities and provide adequate climate finance and technology transfer to developing nations.
• India highlighted its ambitious domestic climate actions, including renewable energy targets and afforestation programs, despite being a developing economy.
• The upcoming COP30 is expected to focus on accelerating emissions reductions, enhancing adaptation measures, and securing robust climate finance commitments.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
keyTerms: United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC)
Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsPoints: COP30: 30th Conference of Parties to UNFCCC.
India's stance: Emphasizes climate justice and CBDR-RC.
Demands: Developed countries to provide climate finance and technology transfer.
India's actions: Ambitious renewable energy targets, afforestation.
Focus of COP30: Emissions reductions, adaptation, climate finance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of 'climate justice' and its relevance in international climate negotiations, with a focus on India's perspective.
Analyze the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) and its implications for developed and developing nations.
Evaluate the challenges in securing adequate climate finance and technology transfer from developed to developing countries.
Examine India's domestic climate actions and its role as a voice for the Global South in advocating for equitable climate solutions.
articles.imageDescription: Globe with a green leaf emerging, symbolizing climate action and environmental protection.
articles.trivia: The UNFCCC was adopted at the Rio Earth Summit in 1992 and entered into force in 1994.

articles.headline: PLI Scheme Expanded to Boost Domestic Production of Critical Minerals
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the expansion of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme to include critical minerals, aiming to reduce import dependence and boost domestic extraction and processing.
• This strategic move targets minerals like lithium, cobalt, nickel, and rare earth elements, essential for advanced electronics, electric vehicles, and renewable energy technologies.
• The expanded PLI scheme will offer financial incentives to companies investing in the entire value chain, from exploration to refining and manufacturing finished products.
• The initiative is crucial for India's energy transition, national security, and achieving self-reliance in key strategic sectors.
• It is expected to attract significant private investment, foster technological innovation, and create high-skilled jobs.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme expanded to include critical minerals.
Targeted minerals: Lithium, cobalt, nickel, rare earth elements.
Objective: Reduce import dependence, boost domestic extraction and processing.
Significance: Energy transition, national security, self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat).
Impact: Attract private investment, foster innovation, job creation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of critical minerals for India's economic growth, energy transition, and national security.
Discuss how the expanded PLI scheme for critical minerals aims to address India's import dependence and build a robust domestic supply chain.
Examine the challenges and opportunities associated with the exploration, extraction, and processing of critical minerals in India.
Evaluate the potential impact of this initiative on India's manufacturing sector, electric vehicle industry, and renewable energy goals.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of various critical minerals (lithium, cobalt) arranged around a circuit board, symbolizing their use in technology.
articles.trivia: India currently imports a significant portion of its critical mineral requirements, making it vulnerable to supply chain disruptions.

articles.headline: Commemoration of 75 Years of India's Republic: Special Events Planned
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture announced a year-long series of special events and initiatives to commemorate the 75th anniversary of India becoming a Republic.
• The celebrations aim to highlight India's democratic journey, constitutional values, and achievements across various sectors since 1950.
• Events will include exhibitions on constitutional history, cultural festivals showcasing India's diversity, and educational programs for youth.
• A key focus will be on promoting awareness about fundamental rights and duties, and the contributions of freedom fighters and constitution makers.
• The commemorations will culminate in a grand Republic Day parade in 2026, showcasing India's progress and cultural heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Significant events, personalities, issues.
prelimsPoints: 75th anniversary of India becoming a Republic (since 1950).
Ministry of Culture planning year-long events.
Focus: Democratic journey, constitutional values, achievements.
Activities: Exhibitions, cultural festivals, educational programs.
Culmination: Republic Day parade 2026.
articles.imageDescription: Stylized '75' with the Indian tricolor and Ashoka Chakra, representing 75 years of the Republic.
articles.trivia: India adopted its Constitution on November 26, 1949, but it came into effect on January 26, 1950, marking the day India became a Republic.

articles.headline: UNESCO Adds New Indian Sites to Tentative World Heritage List
articles.explainer:
• UNESCO announced the inclusion of three new Indian sites to its Tentative List of World Heritage Sites, paving the way for their potential nomination to the prestigious list.
• The newly added sites include a unique ancient rock-cut cave complex, a historic river island known for its cultural landscape, and a distinctive architectural ensemble from a medieval kingdom.
• Inclusion in the Tentative List is the first step in the nomination process, requiring detailed documentation and justification of their Outstanding Universal Value (OUV).
• This recognition highlights India's rich cultural and natural heritage and underscores the efforts towards their preservation and promotion.
• India currently has 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, with several more under consideration, reflecting its diverse heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
keyTerms: ICOMOS (International Council on Monuments and Sites)
prelimsPoints: UNESCO Tentative List: Three new Indian sites added.
Types of sites: Rock-cut cave complex, river island (cultural landscape), medieval architectural ensemble.
Process: Tentative List is first step for World Heritage Site nomination.
Significance: Highlights India's heritage, preservation efforts.
Current total: India has 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites.
articles.imageDescription: Collage of images showing ancient rock-cut caves, a river island, and a medieval palace, representing diverse heritage.
articles.trivia: The Ajanta Caves, Ellora Caves, and Agra Fort were among the first sites in India to be inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List in 1983.

articles.headline: India's Tourism Sector Sees Record Growth, Focus on Sustainable Practices
articles.explainer:
• India's tourism sector recorded an unprecedented surge in both domestic and international tourist arrivals over the past year, contributing significantly to economic recovery.
• The Ministry of Tourism attributed the growth to government initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh' and enhanced connectivity, along with promotion of diverse tourist destinations.
• A new policy framework was announced, emphasizing sustainable and responsible tourism practices to minimize environmental impact and promote local communities.
• Focus areas include eco-tourism, rural tourism, and adventure tourism, with incentives for green infrastructure and community-based tourism projects.
• The sector is projected to create millions of new jobs and attract substantial investment, positioning India as a global tourism hotspot.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Indian tourism sector: Record growth in domestic and international arrivals.
Drivers: 'Dekho Apna Desh' initiative, enhanced connectivity, diverse promotions.
New policy focus: Sustainable and responsible tourism practices.
Key areas: Eco-tourism, rural tourism, adventure tourism.
Impact: Job creation, investment, global tourism hotspot.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted role of the tourism sector in India's economic development, employment generation, and cultural exchange.
Analyze the challenges faced by India's tourism industry and the measures being taken by the government to promote sustainable and inclusive tourism.
Examine how initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh' and focus on niche tourism segments are contributing to the sector's growth and diversification.
Evaluate the potential of tourism to foster local entrepreneurship and preserve cultural heritage while addressing environmental concerns.
articles.imageDescription: Diverse Indian tourist attractions (Taj Mahal, Himalayas, beaches) converging into a single vibrant image.
articles.trivia: The 'Dekho Apna Desh' initiative was launched by the Ministry of Tourism to encourage citizens to travel within the country and explore its diverse destinations.

articles.headline: India Proposes New Framework for AI Ethics and Governance
articles.explainer:
• India's Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) unveiled a draft national framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics and governance.
• The framework aims to ensure responsible development and deployment of AI technologies, focusing on principles of fairness, transparency, accountability, and privacy.
• It proposes the establishment of a National AI Ethics Council and sector-specific AI regulatory bodies to oversee implementation and address emerging challenges.
• The initiative seeks to balance innovation with societal well-being, mitigate risks like algorithmic bias, and protect fundamental rights in the age of AI.
• This move positions India among leading nations developing comprehensive strategies for AI governance.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
prelimsPoints: MeitY's draft framework: AI ethics and governance.
Principles: Fairness, transparency, accountability, privacy.
Proposed bodies: National AI Ethics Council, sector-specific regulatory bodies.
Objectives: Balance innovation with societal well-being, mitigate algorithmic bias, protect rights.
India's position: Among leading nations in AI governance strategy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical challenges posed by the rapid advancement of Artificial Intelligence and the need for a robust governance framework in India.
Analyze the key principles proposed in India's draft AI ethics and governance framework and their potential impact on AI development and deployment.
Examine how algorithmic bias and data privacy concerns can be addressed within the proposed regulatory structure to ensure equitable and inclusive AI adoption.
Compare India's approach to AI governance with international best practices and suggest measures for effective implementation and enforcement.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract image of a human brain merging with a circuit board, symbolizing AI and ethical considerations.
articles.trivia: India is a signatory to the Global Partnership on Artificial Intelligence (GPAI), an international initiative to support responsible and human-centric development of AI.

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) Gains Global Recognition
articles.explainer:
• India's robust Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI), encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, received widespread acclaim at a global summit on digital transformation.
• Several developing nations expressed keen interest in adopting components of India's DPI model to accelerate their own digital inclusion and economic growth.
• The success of UPI (Unified Payments Interface) in fostering financial inclusion and driving digital transactions was particularly highlighted.
• India emphasized its commitment to sharing its open-source DPI solutions with the world, promoting a collaborative approach to digital development.
• This recognition solidifies India's position as a leader in building scalable and inclusive digital ecosystems.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: India's DPI components: Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker.
Global recognition: Widespread acclaim at digital transformation summit.
Interest from: Developing nations for digital inclusion, economic growth.
UPI success: Financial inclusion, digital transactions.
India's commitment: Sharing open-source DPI solutions globally.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and how India's model (India Stack) has driven unprecedented digital transformation and financial inclusion.
Analyze the key features and benefits of Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker as pillars of India's DPI, highlighting their impact on governance and citizen services.
Examine the potential for India's DPI model to be replicated in other developing countries, considering the challenges and opportunities of technology transfer.
Evaluate the strategic implications of India becoming a global leader in providing scalable and inclusive digital solutions, especially for the Global South.
articles.imageDescription: Interconnected digital network lines with icons representing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, encircling a map of India.
articles.trivia: The Unified Payments Interface (UPI) recorded over 11 billion transactions in a single month in 2024, showcasing its massive adoption.

highlights: India launches 'Vision 2047: Advanced Manufacturing Hub' initiative, targeting global leadership in sectors like semiconductors and EVs.
Canada designates Lawrence Bishnoi Gang as a terrorist organisation, enhancing India-Canada cooperation against transnational crime.
ISRO successfully tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV-TD), taking a significant step towards cost-effective space access.
Supreme Court upholds state's power to regulate online gaming involving stakes, providing clarity for the industry.
India achieves a major milestone, crossing 200 GW of installed renewable energy capacity, reinforcing its climate commitments.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Vision 2047: Advanced Manufacturing Hub' initiative, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to transform India into a global advanced manufacturing hub by 2047.
2. It focuses on integrating technologies like AI, IoT, and robotics into traditional industries.
3. The initiative primarily targets the service sector for job creation.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the initiative is explicitly launched with the goal of making India a global advanced manufacturing hub by 2047. Statement 2 is correct as it focuses on integrating cutting-edge technologies like AI, IoT, robotics, and additive manufacturing. Statement 3 is incorrect as the initiative primarily targets the manufacturing sector for job creation and economic growth, not the service sector.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for deciding the benchmark repo rate in India?
options: (b) Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
(c) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is entrusted with the task of fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target while keeping in mind the objective of growth.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Forest (Conservation) Amendment Bill, 2025:
1. It seeks to exempt certain forest lands along international borders from strict conservation rules.
2. The original Forest (Conservation) Act was enacted in 1950.
3. The amendments are primarily aimed at promoting eco-tourism.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the Bill seeks to exempt forest land up to 100 km along international borders and land for national security projects. Statement 2 is incorrect; the original Forest (Conservation) Act was enacted in 1980, not 1950. Statement 3 is incorrect; the amendments are primarily aimed at streamlining approvals for strategic national projects and infrastructure development, not eco-tourism.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following are components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) that have gained global recognition?
1. Aadhaar
2. Goods and Services Tax (GST) Network
3. Unified Payments Interface (UPI)
4. DigiLocker
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker are key components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) or 'India Stack' that have received global acclaim for driving digital inclusion and transformation. The GST Network, while a significant digital initiative, is not typically categorized under the core DPI components like identity, payments, and data exchange layers.

prelimsMocks.question:
With respect to India's position on UN Security Council reforms, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. India advocates for an increase in both permanent and non-permanent members.
2. India emphasizes the disproportionate representation of the global North in the current structure.
3. India seeks a permanent seat in the UNSC.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; India consistently calls for an expanded Security Council with more permanent and non-permanent members. Statement 2 is incorrect; India emphasizes the disproportionate representation of the *global South* and the lack of permanent representation for Africa and Latin America. Statement 3 is correct; India has been a strong contender and advocate for a permanent seat in the UNSC.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze India's 'Vision 2047: Advanced Manufacturing Hub' initiative. Discuss the opportunities it presents for economic growth and self-reliance, as well as the key challenges in its implementation.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Vision 2047 initiative and its goals. Discuss opportunities such as job creation, export boost, technological leapfrogging, and reducing import dependence (e.g., in semiconductors). Elaborate on challenges like skill gap, infrastructure bottlenecks, R&D investment, ease of doing business, and global competition. Conclude with measures for successful implementation.

mainsMocks.question: The recent designation of the Lawrence Bishnoi Gang as a terrorist organisation by Canada highlights the complex interplay between transnational organized crime and internal security. Discuss the challenges posed by such groups to India's security and sovereignty, and suggest strategies for enhanced international cooperation to combat them.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security; Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism. GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by explaining the context of the designation. Detail challenges like cross-border crime (drug trafficking, extortion), radicalization, links to extremist groups, and diplomatic strain. Propose strategies like strengthening intelligence sharing, mutual legal assistance treaties, joint operations, capacity building for law enforcement, and leveraging international forums like Interpol and FATF. Conclude by emphasizing the need for a comprehensive and coordinated global response.

mainsMocks.question: India's push for comprehensive reforms of the UN Security Council is driven by its aspiration for a greater global role and the need for a more representative body. Examine the arguments for and against the expansion of the UNSC, and evaluate India's prospects for securing a permanent seat.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's consistent demand for UNSC reforms. Present arguments for reform: outdated structure, lack of representation (especially Global South), limited effectiveness. Present arguments against expansion: dilution of P5 power, complexity of decision-making, difficulty in consensus. Discuss India's qualifications (democracy, economy, population, UN contributions) and the challenges (rivalries, lack of consensus among P5, G4 opposition). Conclude with a realistic assessment of India's prospects and the diplomatic efforts required.

quickBytes.text: India's renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro) now exceeds 200 GW.
The Forest (Conservation) Act was originally enacted in 1980.
ISRO's RLV-TD program aims to develop a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) reusable launch vehicle.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has six members: three from RBI and three external.
India has 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, with more recently added to the Tentative List.
The 'Dekho Apna Desh' initiative promotes domestic tourism in India.
Gambling falls under the State List (List II) of the Seventh Schedule of the Indian Constitution.
The Global Partnership on Artificial Intelligence (GPAI) promotes responsible AI development.
India's DPI includes Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker for digital inclusion.
The BSF is India's primary border guarding force on its borders with Pakistan and Bangladesh.

GS1.headline: Renowned Filmmaker Yashwant Sardeshpande Passes Away at 62
GS1.explainer:
• Veteran Indian filmmaker Yashwant Sardeshpande passed away at the age of 62 after a prolonged illness, leaving behind a legacy of critically acclaimed works.
• Sardeshpande was known for his distinctive narrative style, often exploring social realism and human relationships with profound depth and sensitivity.
• His filmography includes several regional language films that garnered national awards and international recognition, highlighting diverse cultural nuances of India.
• He was a recipient of multiple National Film Awards for his contributions to Indian cinema, particularly in screenwriting and direction.
• His demise marks a significant loss for the Indian film fraternity and the broader cultural landscape.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
prelimsPoints: Yashwant Sardeshpande: Renowned Indian filmmaker.
Known for: Social realism, human relationships, regional language films.
Awards: Multiple National Film Awards.
Contribution: Significant impact on Indian cinema, especially regional films.
GS1.imageDescription: Black and white portrait of a thoughtful man, symbolizing the late filmmaker.
GS1.trivia: The National Film Awards, established in 1954, are among the most prestigious film awards in India.

GS1.headline: Commemoration of 75 Years of India's Republic: Special Events Planned
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture announced a year-long series of special events and initiatives to commemorate the 75th anniversary of India becoming a Republic.
• The celebrations aim to highlight India's democratic journey, constitutional values, and achievements across various sectors since 1950.
• Events will include exhibitions on constitutional history, cultural festivals showcasing India's diversity, and educational programs for youth.
• A key focus will be on promoting awareness about fundamental rights and duties, and the contributions of freedom fighters and constitution makers.
• The commemorations will culminate in a grand Republic Day parade in 2026, showcasing India's progress and cultural heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Significant events, personalities, issues.
prelimsPoints: 75th anniversary of India becoming a Republic (since 1950).
Ministry of Culture planning year-long events.
Focus: Democratic journey, constitutional values, achievements.
Activities: Exhibitions, cultural festivals, educational programs.
Culmination: Republic Day parade 2026.
GS1.imageDescription: Stylized '75' with the Indian tricolor and Ashoka Chakra, representing 75 years of the Republic.
GS1.trivia: India adopted its Constitution on November 26, 1949, but it came into effect on January 26, 1950, marking the day India became a Republic.

GS1.headline: UNESCO Adds New Indian Sites to Tentative World Heritage List
GS1.explainer:
• UNESCO announced the inclusion of three new Indian sites to its Tentative List of World Heritage Sites, paving the way for their potential nomination to the prestigious list.
• The newly added sites include a unique ancient rock-cut cave complex, a historic river island known for its cultural landscape, and a distinctive architectural ensemble from a medieval kingdom.
• Inclusion in the Tentative List is the first step in the nomination process, requiring detailed documentation and justification of their Outstanding Universal Value (OUV).
• This recognition highlights India's rich cultural and natural heritage and underscores the efforts towards their preservation and promotion.
• India currently has 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, with several more under consideration, reflecting its diverse heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
keyTerms: ICOMOS (International Council on Monuments and Sites)
prelimsPoints: UNESCO Tentative List: Three new Indian sites added.
Types of sites: Rock-cut cave complex, river island (cultural landscape), medieval architectural ensemble.
Process: Tentative List is first step for World Heritage Site nomination.
Significance: Highlights India's heritage, preservation efforts.
Current total: India has 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites.
GS1.imageDescription: Collage of images showing ancient rock-cut caves, a river island, and a medieval palace, representing diverse heritage.
GS1.trivia: The Ajanta Caves, Ellora Caves, and Agra Fort were among the first sites in India to be inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List in 1983.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds State's Power in Regulating Online Gaming
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, upheld the constitutional validity of state laws empowering governments to regulate and ban online gaming involving stakes.
• The ruling clarified that online games of 'skill' might still fall under state legislative competence if they involve monetary stakes and have potential social impact.
• The Court emphasized the public order and morality aspects, allowing states to frame laws to prevent addiction and financial losses associated with online gambling.
• This decision provides clarity to the burgeoning online gaming industry and state governments grappling with regulatory frameworks.
• It differentiates between games of pure skill without stakes and those involving monetary transactions, which can be regulated under state gambling laws.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court ruling on online gaming: Upholds state's power to regulate/ban if stakes involved.
Distinction: Games of skill vs. games of chance, especially with monetary stakes.
Constitutional aspect: State legislative competence under public order and morality.
Impact: Clarity for online gaming industry and state governments.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on online gaming for India's federal structure and the balance of power between Union and States.
Discuss the socio-economic challenges posed by unregulated online gaming and how state regulations aim to address them.
Critically examine the distinction between 'games of skill' and 'games of chance' in the context of emerging digital platforms and their legal implications.
Suggest a balanced regulatory framework for online gaming in India that promotes innovation while safeguarding public interest.
GS2.imageDescription: Image of a gavel on a law book, symbolizing a Supreme Court judgment, with abstract digital gaming elements.
GS2.trivia: Gambling is primarily a subject under the State List (List II) of the Seventh Schedule of the Indian Constitution.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded with both nations reaffirming their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defense cooperation, including joint military exercises, technology transfer, and maritime security partnerships.
• Economic cooperation saw new proposals for investment in India's infrastructure, digital transformation, and clean energy sectors.
• Both sides expressed concerns over regional stability and emphasized the importance of multilateralism and adherence to international law.
• The dialogue also covered collaboration in critical and emerging technologies, supply chain resilience, and cooperation in third countries.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue: Annual event, focus on Indo-Pacific.
Key areas of cooperation: Defense, economic investment, digital transformation, clean energy, critical technologies.
Shared vision: Free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
Relevance: Multilateralism, international law, regional stability.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-Japan Special Strategic and Global Partnership in shaping the geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the various pillars of cooperation between India and Japan, including defense, economy, and technology, and their strategic implications for India.
Examine how India and Japan are collaborating to address common challenges such as maritime security, supply chain resilience, and climate change.
Evaluate the role of India-Japan cooperation in strengthening multilateral frameworks like the Quad and promoting regional stability.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan side-by-side, with a stylized map of the Indo-Pacific region in the background.
GS2.trivia: Japan is one of India's largest providers of Official Development Assistance (ODA), funding major infrastructure projects like the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.

GS2.headline: Parliament Passes Bill to Amend Forest Conservation Act for Strategic Projects
GS2.explainer:
• Parliament passed the Forest (Conservation) Amendment Bill, 2025, which aims to streamline approvals for diverting forest land for strategic national projects.
• The Bill seeks to exempt certain categories of land, including forest land up to 100 km along international borders and land used for national security projects, from strict conservation rules.
• Proponents argue the amendments are crucial for infrastructure development, national security, and facilitating ease of doing business.
• Critics express concerns that the relaxed provisions could lead to significant deforestation and undermine environmental protection efforts.
• The Act, originally enacted in 1980, mandated prior approval from the central government for any non-forest use of forest land.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Forest (Conservation) Amendment Bill, 2025: Passed by Parliament.
Purpose: Streamline approvals for strategic national projects on forest land.
Exemptions: Land up to 100 km along international borders, national security projects.
Arguments: Infrastructure, national security vs. environmental concerns.
Original Act: Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the Forest (Conservation) Amendment Bill, 2025, in the context of balancing environmental protection with developmental and national security imperatives.
Discuss the potential ecological and social impacts of diluting forest conservation laws, particularly concerning tribal rights and biodiversity.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing environmental safeguards and compensatory afforestation mechanisms in mitigating the adverse effects of forest diversion.
Propose alternative approaches or stricter implementation strategies that can reconcile developmental needs with robust forest conservation.
GS2.imageDescription: Graphic showing a balance scale with 'Development' on one side and 'Environment' on the other, with a forest background.
GS2.trivia: The Forest (Conservation) Act of 1980 was enacted to check deforestation and ensure that forest land is not diverted for non-forest purposes without central government approval.

GS2.headline: Government Launches National Digital Health Mission (NDHM) Phase 2
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry officially rolled out Phase 2 of the National Digital Health Mission (NDHM), expanding its reach and functionalities across the country.
• Phase 2 focuses on integrating more healthcare providers, diagnostic labs, and pharmacies into the digital ecosystem, along with advanced telemedicine services.
• Key features include a more robust Health ID system, enhanced data privacy and security protocols, and interoperability standards for seamless data exchange.
• The mission aims to create a comprehensive digital health infrastructure, improving access, efficiency, and quality of healthcare services.
• Special emphasis is placed on leveraging AI and machine learning for predictive analytics and personalized healthcare solutions.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: National Digital Health Mission (NDHM) Phase 2 launched.
Focus: Integrating more providers, advanced telemedicine, robust Health ID.
Goal: Comprehensive digital health infrastructure, improve access, efficiency, quality.
Technology: AI and ML for predictive analytics, personalized healthcare.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of the National Digital Health Mission (NDHM) to transform healthcare delivery in India, addressing issues of access, affordability, and quality.
Analyze the challenges involved in implementing a large-scale digital health infrastructure, including data privacy, cybersecurity, and digital literacy.
Examine how NDHM aligns with other government initiatives like Ayushman Bharat and the broader vision of digital India.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to digital health services across rural and urban areas and for vulnerable populations.
GS2.imageDescription: Abstract graphic of interconnected medical symbols and digital lines, representing digital health network.
GS2.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), launched in 2021, is the current name for the National Digital Health Mission.

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Comprehensive UN Security Council Reforms at General Assembly
GS2.explainer:
• India reiterated its strong call for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) during the ongoing UN General Assembly session.
• India emphasized the need for an expanded Security Council that reflects contemporary global realities, including an increase in both permanent and non-permanent members.
• The permanent representative highlighted the disproportionate representation of the global South and the lack of permanent representation for Africa and Latin America.
• India's consistent demand for a permanent seat aligns with its growing global stature and its contributions to international peace and security.
• The push for reforms aims to make the UNSC more democratic, representative, effective, and legitimate in addressing 21st-century challenges.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India's stance on UNSC: Call for comprehensive reforms, expansion of permanent and non-permanent members.
Reason: Reflect contemporary global realities, address underrepresentation of Global South (Africa, Latin America).
India's demand: Permanent seat in UNSC.
Objective: Make UNSC more democratic, representative, effective, legitimate.
mainsPoints: Discuss the arguments for and against the reform of the UN Security Council, with particular reference to India's aspirations for a permanent seat.
Analyze how the current structure of the UNSC impacts its effectiveness in addressing contemporary global challenges and representing diverse geopolitical interests.
Examine the implications of a reformed UNSC for global governance and the balance of power among nations.
Evaluate the diplomatic strategies employed by India to garner support for its permanent membership bid and the challenges it faces.
GS2.imageDescription: The United Nations building in New York, with flags of member states, symbolizing global diplomacy.
GS2.trivia: The UN Security Council has 5 permanent members (China, France, Russia, UK, US) and 10 non-permanent members elected for two-year terms.

GS3.headline: India Unveils 'Vision 2047: Advanced Manufacturing Hub' Initiative
GS3.explainer:
• The Prime Minister launched a new national initiative aimed at transforming India into a global advanced manufacturing hub by 2047, coinciding with 100 years of independence.
• The initiative focuses on integrating cutting-edge technologies like AI, IoT, robotics, and additive manufacturing into traditional industries.
• Key pillars include skill development for future industries, robust R&D infrastructure, and attracting significant foreign and domestic investment.
• It seeks to enhance India's competitiveness in critical sectors such as semiconductors, electric vehicles, aerospace, and pharmaceuticals.
• Special economic zones with advanced manufacturing capabilities and incentives for green manufacturing are also part of the plan.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Industrial policy
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Vision 2047: India's target for becoming a developed nation.
Advanced Manufacturing Hub initiative components: AI, IoT, robotics, skill development, R&D.
Key sectors targeted: semiconductors, EVs, aerospace, pharmaceuticals.
Role of Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes in boosting manufacturing.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's strategy to leverage advanced manufacturing for economic growth and job creation, linking it to the 'Make in India' initiative.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in integrating Industry 4.0 technologies into India's manufacturing sector.
Evaluate the potential impact of such initiatives on India's global supply chain resilience and export competitiveness.
Examine the role of government policy, skill development, and R&D in fostering an advanced manufacturing ecosystem.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration depicting smart factories with robots and AI interfaces, symbolizing advanced manufacturing.
GS3.trivia: India's manufacturing sector contributes approximately 17% to the nation's GDP, with ambitious targets for significant growth.

GS3.headline: Canada Designates Lawrence Bishnoi Gang as Terrorist Organisation
GS3.explainer:
• The Canadian government officially designated the Lawrence Bishnoi Gang as a terrorist organization, citing its involvement in transnational organized crime, violence, and threats against Indian Canadians.
• This move follows increased diplomatic pressure from India and a growing concern over the gang's alleged activities, including extortion, drug trafficking, and targeted killings.
• The designation allows Canadian law enforcement agencies to impose stricter measures, including asset freezes, travel bans, and enhanced surveillance against the group and its affiliates.
• India had repeatedly raised concerns about the gang's operations from Canadian soil, particularly its alleged links to extremist elements and cross-border criminal networks.
• The decision is seen as a significant step in bilateral cooperation between India and Canada on security matters, aiming to curb organized crime and terrorism.
gsSubTopics: Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: Lawrence Bishnoi Gang: Designated as a terrorist organization by Canada.
Reasons for designation: Transnational organized crime, violence, threats against Indian Canadians.
Implications of designation: Asset freezes, travel bans, enhanced surveillance.
Context: India-Canada bilateral security cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of transnational organized crime groups operating from foreign soil on India's internal security and diplomatic relations.
Discuss the challenges and mechanisms for international cooperation in combating organized crime and terrorism, with specific reference to India-Canada relations.
Examine the role of diplomatic pressure and intelligence sharing in addressing cross-border criminal activities.
Evaluate the effectiveness of terrorist designations by foreign governments in curbing the activities of such groups.
GS3.imageDescription: Map showing Canada and India, connected by lines representing international cooperation against crime.
GS3.trivia: Interpol plays a crucial role in facilitating international police cooperation and combating transnational crime.

GS3.headline: India Achieves Significant Milestone in Renewable Energy Capacity Addition
GS3.explainer:
• India announced achieving a major milestone by crossing 200 GW of installed renewable energy capacity, excluding large hydro, well ahead of its initial targets.
• This achievement underscores the nation's aggressive push towards green energy and its commitment to climate change mitigation.
• Solar and wind power constitute the largest share of this capacity, driven by supportive government policies like Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes and competitive bidding.
• The accelerated deployment of renewable energy is crucial for India to meet its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
• The focus now shifts towards grid modernization, energy storage solutions, and green hydrogen integration to ensure grid stability and further decarbonization.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India's renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro) crosses 200 GW.
Major contributors: Solar and Wind power.
Government policies: PLI schemes, competitive bidding.
Relevance: Meeting NDCs under Paris Agreement, climate change mitigation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's rapid growth in renewable energy capacity and its implications for energy security and environmental sustainability.
Discuss the challenges associated with integrating high shares of intermittent renewable energy into the national grid and the solutions being explored (e.g., storage, smart grids).
Evaluate India's role in global climate action, specifically its progress towards its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) and future targets.
Examine the economic opportunities and policy framework required to further accelerate India's energy transition, including green hydrogen.
GS3.imageDescription: Panoramic view of a large solar power farm and wind turbines under a clear sky.
GS3.trivia: India is the third-largest emitter of greenhouse gases globally, making its renewable energy transition critical for global climate efforts.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV-TD)
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducted a crucial test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD).
• This test involved a precise landing experiment, simulating the return of a reusable launch vehicle from space, a key step towards India's future space capabilities.
• The RLV-TD program aims to develop a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch vehicle, significantly reducing the cost of space access.
• This technology is vital for future missions, including human spaceflight (Gaganyaan) and deploying large satellite constellations.
• The successful test positions India among a select few nations developing advanced reusable space technology.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD: Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator.
Purpose: Reduce cost of space access, develop two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) vehicle.
Latest test: Precise landing experiment, simulating re-entry.
Significance: Crucial for future missions like Gaganyaan, positions India in advanced space tech.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of reusable launch vehicle technology for India's space program and its implications for commercial space exploration.
Analyze how ISRO's RLV-TD program aligns with India's broader vision for technological self-reliance and global leadership in space.
Examine the economic and scientific benefits of reducing the cost of access to space through reusable technology.
Evaluate the challenges and future prospects for India in developing and deploying fully reusable launch systems.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV-TD) making a controlled descent.
GS3.trivia: The first successful test of the RLV-TD's hypersonic flight experiment was conducted in May 2016.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at its latest meeting.
• The decision was driven by signs of easing retail inflation and a need to support economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
• RBI indicated a cautious stance, monitoring food inflation and global commodity prices, which remain potential risks.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to achieving the medium-term target for consumer price index (CPI) inflation of 4% within a band of +/- 2%.
• Experts anticipate that this pause will provide stability to lending rates and encourage investment, contributing to a gradual economic recovery.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy.
prelimsPoints: RBI MPC decision: Repo rate maintained.
Reason: Easing retail inflation, support economic growth, global uncertainties.
RBI's inflation target: 4% CPI with +/- 2% band.
Impact: Stability in lending rates, potential for investment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing the RBI's monetary policy decisions, particularly the trade-off between inflation control and economic growth.
Discuss the effectiveness of the repo rate as a tool for managing inflation and liquidity in the Indian economy.
Examine the role of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India's economic governance and its impact on financial markets.
Critically evaluate the challenges faced by the RBI in achieving its inflation targets while navigating global economic volatility and domestic supply-side pressures.
GS3.imageDescription: Close-up of Indian currency notes with a magnifying glass, symbolizing economic analysis.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: New Integrated Border Management System (IBMS) Deployed Along Western Border
GS3.explainer:
• India successfully deployed an advanced Integrated Border Management System (IBMS) along critical stretches of its western border with Pakistan.
• The IBMS integrates state-of-the-art surveillance technologies, including thermal imagers, night-vision devices, radar, and underground sensors, with a centralized command and control system.
• The system aims to enhance real-time situational awareness, detect infiltrations more effectively, and reduce human reliance in challenging terrains.
• This deployment is part of a broader strategy to fortify India's borders against cross-border terrorism, smuggling, and illegal immigration.
• Training for border security forces (BSF) personnel has been intensified to operate and maintain the sophisticated technology.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security.
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: IBMS: Integrated Border Management System deployed on western border.
Technologies used: Thermal imagers, night-vision, radar, underground sensors.
Objective: Enhance real-time situational awareness, detect infiltration, reduce human reliance.
Purpose: Fortify borders against terrorism, smuggling, illegal immigration.
